import webbrowser
import time
import os
import sys

print("­¤öŹ SymSafe Voice Input Checker\n")

# 1. Check if running on localhost
print("1’ĖÅŌāŻ Checking URL...")
if len(sys.argv) > 1 and "127.0.0.1" in sys.argv[1]:
    print("ŌØī You're using 127.0.0.1 - Voice input won't work!")
    print("Ō£ģ FIX: Use http://localhost:5000 instead\n")
else:
    print("Ō£ģ Good - Use http://localhost:5000\n")

# 2. Create a test page (without emojis to avoid encoding issues)
test_html = """<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>Voice Test</title>
    <style>
        body { font-family: Arial; padding: 50px; text-align: center; }
        button { font-size: 20px; padding: 15px 30px; margin: 10px; cursor: pointer; }
        #status { font-size: 18px; margin: 20px; padding: 20px; background: #f0f0f0; }
        .success { color: green; font-weight: bold; }
        .error { color: red; font-weight: bold; }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>SymSafe Voice Test</h1>
    <button onclick="testVoice()">Click to Test Microphone</button>
    <button onclick="checkBrowser()">Check Browser Support</button>
    <div id="status">Status will appear here...</div>
    
    <script>
    function checkBrowser() {
        let msg = "";
        if ('webkitSpeechRecognition' in window) {
            msg = "SUCCESS: Your browser supports voice input!";
        } else if ('SpeechRecognition' in window) {
            msg = "SUCCESS: Your browser supports voice input!";
        } else {
            msg = "ERROR: Your browser doesn't support voice input. Use Chrome or Edge.";
        }
        document.getElementById('status').innerHTML = msg;
    }
    
    function testVoice() {
        const status = document.getElementById('status');
        
        if (!('webkitSpeechRecognition' in window) && !('SpeechRecognition' in window)) {
            status.innerHTML = '<span class="error">ERROR: Speech recognition not supported in this browser!</span>';
            return;
        }
        
        const recognition = new (window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition)();
        
        recognition.onstart = () => {
            status.innerHTML = 'LISTENING... Say something!';
        };
        
        recognition.onresult = (event) => {
            const transcript = event.results[0][0].transcript;
            status.innerHTML = '<span class="success">SUCCESS! You said: "' + transcript + '"</span>';
        };
        
        recognition.onerror = (event) => {
            let errorMsg = '<span class="error">ERROR: ';
            switch(event.error) {
                case 'not-allowed':
                    errorMsg += 'Microphone permission denied. Please allow microphone access and reload the page.</span>';
                    break;
                case 'no-speech':
                    errorMsg += 'No speech detected. Try again!</span>';
                    break;
                default:
                    errorMsg += event.error + '</span>';
            }
            status.innerHTML = errorMsg;
        };
        
        try {
            recognition.start();
        } catch(e) {
            status.innerHTML = '<span class="error">ERROR: Failed to start: ' + e.message + '</span>';
        }
    }
    </script>
</body>
</html>"""

# Save test file with UTF-8 encoding
with open("voice_test.html", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write(test_html)

print("2’ĖÅŌāŻ Created voice_test.html")

# 3. Open in browser
print("3’ĖÅŌāŻ Opening test page in your browser...")
webbrowser.open(f"file:///{os.path.abspath('voice_test.html')}")

print("\n­¤ōŗ INSTRUCTIONS:")
print("1. Click 'Check Browser Support' first")
print("2. Then click 'Click to Test Microphone'")
print("3. Allow microphone access if prompted")
print("4. Say something clearly")
print("\n­¤ö¦ COMMON FIXES:")
print("- Use Chrome or Edge browser")
print("- Make sure you're on http://localhost:5000 (not 127.0.0.1)")
print("- Allow microphone permissions when prompted")
print("\nŌ£ģ If the test works, your voice input should work in SymSafe too!")



===== C:\Users\travi\OneDrive\$projects\HC-AI\symsafe-ai\debug_match.py =====
import json
import difflib
import re

SYMPTOM_TREE_PATH = "prompts/symptom_tree.json"

def load_symptom_tree():
    with open(SYMPTOM_TREE_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        return json.load(f)

def normalize_text(text):
    return re.sub(r"[^\w\s]", "", text.lower().strip())

def fuzzy_match_symptom(user_input, symptom_tree, threshold=0.6):
    normalized_input = normalize_text(user_input)
    candidates = {}
    for canonical, entry in symptom_tree.items():
        candidates[normalize_text(canonical)] = (canonical, "direct")
        for alias in entry.get("aliases", []):
            candidates[normalize_text(alias)] = (canonical, "alias")

    matches = difflib.get_close_matches(normalized_input, candidates.keys(), n=1, cutoff=threshold)
    return candidates[matches[0]] if matches else (None, None), normalized_input, matches, list(candidates.keys())

# --- RUN TEST ---
symptom_tree = load_symptom_tree()
test_input = "I keep throwing up"

(match, match_type), norm_input, matches, candidate_keys = fuzzy_match_symptom(test_input, symptom_tree)

print("\n­¤¦¬ DEBUG MATCH RESULTS")
print(f"Input: {test_input}")
print(f"Normalized: {norm_input}")
print(f"Matched Symptom: {match} (type: {match_type})")
print(f"Match Candidates: {matches}")
print(f"Total Indexed Phrases: {len(candidate_keys)}")



===== C:\Users\travi\OneDrive\$projects\HC-AI\symsafe-ai\dump_exports.py =====
import requests

BASE = "http://127.0.0.1:5000"

def check_csv(path):
    print(f"\n­¤öŹ Checking {path}")
    try:
        r = requests.get(BASE + path)
        print(f"Status Code: {r.status_code}")
        print(f"Content Length: {len(r.content)} bytes")
        content = r.content.decode("utf-8").strip()
        print("---- CSV Content ----")
        print(content if content else "(empty)")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"ŌØī Error fetching {path}: {e}")

check_csv("/download-tests")
check_csv("/download-reviews")



===== C:\Users\travi\OneDrive\$projects\HC-AI\symsafe-ai\fix_encoding_bom.py =====
# fix_encoding_bom.py
files = [
    "prompts/test_cases.json",
    "prompts/learning_log.json",
    "prompts/gpt_review_log.json",
    "prompts/symptom_tree.json"
]

for path in files:
    with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
        content = f.read()
    with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(content)
    print(f"Ō£ģ Cleaned BOM from {path}")

import json

file_path = "prompts/test_cases.json"

with open(file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
    data = json.load(f)

with open(file_path, "w", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
    json.dump(data, f, indent=2)

print("Ō£ģ test_cases.json re-encoded cleanly with utf-8-sig")

      <button class="voice-mode-btn" onclick="toggleVoiceMode()" id="voiceModeBtn">
        <span>?? Voice Mode</span>
      </button>
    </div>
    
    <!-- Messages Area -->
    <div class="chat-messages" id="chatMessages">
      <div class="welcome-message" id="welcomeMessage">
        <h2>Welcome to SymSafe AI</h2>
        <p>I'm here to help you understand your symptoms and guide you to appropriate care.</p>
        <p class="text-muted">Remember: I'm not a doctor and cannot diagnose. Always consult a healthcare provider for medical advice.</p>
        
        <div class="example-symptoms">
          <p>Try saying or typing:</p>
          <button onclick="sendExample('I have a headache')">I have a headache</button>
          <button onclick="sendExample('I feel dizzy')">I feel dizzy</button>
          <button onclick="sendExample('My child has a fever')">My child has a fever</button>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
    
    <!-- Input Area -->
    <div class="chat-input">
      <input 
        type="text" 
        id="symptomInput" 
        placeholder="Type your symptom or question..." 
        onkeypress="handleKeyPress(event)"
      >
      <button onclick="sendMessage()" id="sendButton">
        <span>Send</span>
      </button>
      <button onclick="startVoice()" id="voiceButton">
        <span>??</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- Include voice functions -->
  <script src="/static/js/voice-functions.js"></script>

  <script>
    let messageHistory = [];
    
    function handleKeyPress(event) {
      if (event.key === 'Enter') {
        sendMessage();
      }
    }
    
    function sendExample(text) {
      document.getElementById('symptomInput').value = text;
      sendMessage();
    }
    
    function sendMessage() {
      const input = document.getElementById('symptomInput').value.trim();
      if (!input) return;
      
      // Hide welcome message on first interaction
      const welcomeMsg = document.getElementById('welcomeMessage');
      if (welcomeMsg) {
        welcomeMsg.style.display = 'none';
      }
      
      // Add user message to chat
      addMessage(input, 'user');
      
      // Clear input
      document.getElementById('symptomInput').value = '';
      
      // Disable send button temporarily
      document.getElementById('sendButton').disabled = true;
      
      // Send to API
      fetch('/api/symptom', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: {'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
        body: JSON.stringify({ text: input, session_id: sessionId })
      })
      .then(res => res.json())
      .then(data => {
        const response = data.response || "I'm having trouble understanding. Could you please rephrase that?";
        addMessage(response, 'assistant');
        
        // Speak response if voice is enabled
        if (voiceEnabled) {
          speakResponse(response);
        
        // If waiting for answer, prepare to listen immediately
        window.awaitingResponse = data.awaiting_response;
        if (data.awaiting_response && continuousMode && voiceEnabled) {
            // Set a flag that we're waiting for speech to end
            window.speechSynthesis.speaking = true;
            
            // Check every 50ms if speech has ended
            const checkSpeechEnd = setInterval(() => {
                if (!window.speechSynthesis.speaking && !window.speechSynthesis.pending && !isListening) {
                    clearInterval(checkSpeechEnd);
                    startVoice();
                }
            }, 50);
        }
        
        // If system asked a question, keep listening
        window.awaitingResponse = data.awaiting_response;
        if (data.awaiting_response && continuousMode && voiceEnabled) {
            setTimeout(() => {
                if (continuousMode && voiceEnabled && !isListening) {
                    startVoice();
                }
            }, 100);
        }
        }
        
        // Re-enable send button
        document.getElementById('sendButton').disabled = false;
        
        // Focus back on input
        document.getElementById('symptomInput').focus();
      })
      .catch(err => {
        addMessage("I'm sorry, I'm having technical difficulties. Please try again.", 'assistant');
        document.getElementById('sendButton').disabled = false;
      });
    }
    
    function addMessage(text, sender) {
      const messagesDiv = document.getElementById('chatMessages');
      const messageDiv = document.createElement('div');
      messageDiv.className = `message ${sender}`;
      
      const time = new Date().toLocaleTimeString([], { hour: '2-digit', minute: '2-digit' });
      
      messageDiv.innerHTML = `
        <div class="message-bubble">
          ${text}
          ${sender === 'assistant' && voiceEnabled ? '<span id="speaking-indicator" class="speaking-indicator" style="display:none;"><span class="pulse"></span> Speaking...</span>' : ''}
          <div class="message-time">${time}</div>
        </div>
      `;
      
      messagesDiv.appendChild(messageDiv);
      
      // Scroll to bottom
      messagesDiv.scrollTop = messagesDiv.scrollHeight;
      
      // Store in history
      messageHistory.push({ text, sender, time });
    }
    
    function startVoice() {if(isListening)return;
      isListening=true;const recognition = new (window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition)();
      recognition.lang = "en-US";
      recognition.interimResults = false;
      recognition.maxAlternatives = 1;
      
      const voiceButton = document.getElementById('voiceButton');
      voiceButton.innerHTML = '<span class="listening-indicator"><span class="pulse"></span> Listening...</span>';
      voiceButton.disabled = true;
      
      recognition.onresult = event => {
        const speech = event.results[0][0].transcript;
        document.getElementById('symptomInput').value = speech;
        sendMessage();
      };
      
      recognition.onerror = event => {
        // Don't show alert for no-speech in continuous mode
        if (event.error !== 'no-speech' || !continuousMode) {
            alert("Voice input error: " + event.error);
        }
        resetVoiceButton();isListening=false;
      };
      
      recognition.onend = event => {
        resetVoiceButton();isListening=false;
      };
      
      recognition.start();
    }
    
    function resetVoiceButton() {
      const voiceButton = document.getElementById('voiceButton');
      voiceButton.innerHTML = '<span>??</span>';
      voiceButton.disabled = false;
    }
    
    // Override the toggleVoice function to update our UI
    const originalToggleVoice = window.toggleVoice;
    window.toggleVoice = function() {
      originalToggleVoice();const continuousToggle=document.getElementById("continuousToggle");if(voiceEnabled){continuousToggle.style.display="flex";}else{continuousToggle.style.display="none";continuousMode=false;document.getElementById("continuousModeSwitch").checked=false;}
      const icon = document.getElementById('voiceIcon');
      const text = document.getElementById('voiceText');
      
      if (voiceEnabled) {
        icon.textContent = '??';
        text.textContent = 'Voice On';
      } else {
        icon.textContent = '??';
        text.textContent = 'Voice Off';
      }
    };
    
    // Focus on input when page loads
    let continuousMode=false;let isListening=false;function toggleContinuousMode(){continuousMode=document.getElementById("continuousModeSwitch").checked;const msg=continuousMode?"Continuous mode ON!":"Continuous mode OFF";const notification=document.createElement("div");notification.style.cssText="position:fixed;top:80px;left:50%;transform:translateX(-50%);background:#25d366;color:white;padding:12px 24px;border-radius:25px;z-index:1000;";notification.textContent=msg;document.body.appendChild(notification);setTimeout(()=>notification.remove(),3000);}// Session tracking
    const sessionId = 'session_' + Date.now() + '_' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9);
    
    
    function toggleVoiceMode() {
      voiceEnabled = !voiceEnabled;
      const btn = document.getElementById('voiceModeBtn');
      
      if (voiceEnabled) {
        // Turn on voice mode AND continuous mode with one click
        continuousMode = true;
        btn.classList.add('active');
        btn.innerHTML = '<span>?? Voice Mode Active</span>';
        
        // Start listening immediately
        startVoice();
      } else {
        // Turn everything off
        continuousMode = false;
        btn.classList.remove('active');
        btn.innerHTML = '<span>?? Voice Mode</span>';
        
        // Stop any ongoing speech
        window.speechSynthesis.cancel();
        
        // Stop recognition if active
        if (typeof recognition !== 'undefined' && recognition) {
          try { recognition.stop(); } catch(e) {}
        }
      }
    }
    
    window.onload = function() {
      document.getElementById('symptomInput').focus();
    };
  </script>
</body>
</html>

import json
import os
import re

# --------------------------
# 1. PATCH /review-gpt route
# --------------------------

target_file = "symsafe_webui.py"
patched_route = """
@app.route("/review-gpt")
def review_gpt():
    try:
        with open(GPT_REVIEW_LOG_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            reviews = json.load(f)
    except:
        reviews = {}

    try:
        with open(LEARNING_LOG_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            learning_log = json.load(f)
    except:
        learning_log = {}

    return render_template("review_gpt.html", reviews=reviews, learning_log=learning_log)
"""

with open(target_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    code = f.read()

code = re.sub(
    r"@app\.route\(\"/review-gpt\"\)[\s\S]*?return render_template\([^)]+\)",
    patched_route.strip(),
    code
)

with open(target_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write(code)

print("Ō£ģ Patched /review-gpt route in symsafe_webui.py")

# --------------------------
# 2. Inject valid data files
# --------------------------

os.makedirs("prompts", exist_ok=True)

with open("prompts/test_cases.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    json.dump([
        {
            "input": "validation test input",
            "expected_symptom": "headache",
            "actual": "headache"
        }
    ], f, indent=2)
print("Ō£ģ Wrote prompts/test_cases.json")

with open("prompts/gpt_review_log.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    json.dump({
        "entry001": {
            "empathy": "Ō£ģ Empathetic",
            "escalation": "ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Vague",
            "diagnosis": "Ō£ģ No diagnosis",
            "clarity": "Ō£ģ Clear & concise",
            "comments": "Good response overall.",
            "flagged": False
        }
    }, f, indent=2)
print("Ō£ģ Wrote prompts/gpt_review_log.json")

with open("prompts/learning_log.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    json.dump({
        "entry001": {
            "input": "my head is pounding",
            "gpt_response": "This may be a headache. Please consult your provider if it continues."
        }
    }, f, indent=2)
print("Ō£ģ Wrote prompts/learning_log.json")

# --------------------------
# 3. Fix admin2.html heading
# --------------------------

admin2_path = os.path.join("templates", "admin2.html")
if os.path.exists(admin2_path):
    with open(admin2_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        html = f.read()
    html = re.sub(r"<h1>.*?</h1>", "<h1>­¤øĀ’ĖÅ Admin Tool Hub</h1>", html)
    with open(admin2_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(html)
    print("Ō£ģ Fixed heading in admin2.html")
else:
    print("ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ templates/admin2.html not found, skipping heading fix")

print("\n­¤Äē All fixes applied. Restart Flask and re-run validator.")

# SymSafe AI ŌĆö v1.1 Project Tracker

This file serves as a living document for tracking SymSafe AI development. Paste this into a future chat to resume work or verify status.

---

## Ō£ģ Current Branch
`v1.1-dev` (local development only, not public)

---

## ­¤¦Ł Project Goal
Refactor and polish all SymSafe AI routes for v1.1 release, making the app fully trainer-usable in the browser with modern UI and audit tools.

---

## Ō£ģ Completed Routes

### `/review-gpt` (tag: `v1.1-dev-review-gpt`)
- [x] Collapsible reviewed entries
- [x] Edit mode with prefilled values
- [x] ­¤Ü® Flag support + red border
- [x] ŌĆ£Show ReviewedŌĆØ + ŌĆ£Show FlaggedŌĆØ filters
- [x] CSV export
- [x] Sticky header with progress bar
- [x] Save confirmation toast
- [x] Full layout polish

Ō£ģ **This page is complete and stable.**

---

## ­¤ö¦ Pages Still To Complete

### `/edit-aliases`
- [ ] Flag editing (e.g. requires escalation)
- [ ] Improved UI layout
- [ ] Inline guidance and response examples

### `/train`
- [ ] Confirm JSON mappings update correctly
- [ ] Trainer UI for mapping unmatched input
- [ ] Create new entries directly from UI

### `/edit-tests`
- [ ] Load + edit `test_cases.json`
- [ ] Validate input structure
- [ ] Add visual schema help

### `/review-tests`
- [ ] Display last QA run markdown
- [ ] Highlight failures
- [ ] Filter: failed only

### `/voice-log`
- [ ] Show voice transcripts
- [ ] Add filter + download
- [ ] Show flagged or retried input

### `/audit-summary`
- [ ] Stats: matched vs GPT fallback
- [ ] Filter by date or escalation
- [ ] Graphs or sparklines for UI polish

---

## ­¤ōī Git Strategy

- Work locally in `v1.1-dev` branch
- After completing each route, run:
  ```
  git add .
  git commit -m "Finish /ROUTE for v1.1"
  git tag v1.1-dev-ROUTE
  ```
- When all pages are complete and verified, tag `v1.1.0` and prepare release notes

|-----|-------------|
| `v1.1-dev-review-gpt` | Finalized `/review-gpt` with all v1.1 polish |

import json
from pathlib import Path

base_path = Path("prompts")
files = {
    "symptom_tree.json": base_path / "symptom_tree.json",
    "test_cases.json": base_path / "test_cases.json",
    "learning_log.json": base_path / "learning_log.json"
}

for name, path in files.items():
    print(f"\n­¤öŹ Checking: {path}")
    if not path.exists():
        print("ŌØī MISSING")
        continue

    try:
        content = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        data = json.loads(content)
        print(f"Ō£ģ {name} loaded successfully ({len(content)} characters, {type(data).__name__})")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"ŌØī Failed to load {name}: {e}")

?? FILE: C:\Users\travi\OneDrive\$projects\HC-AI\symsafe-ai\dev\reset_symsafe_files.py
-----------------------------
import os
import json
import shutil

# Use current working directory as root
PROJECT_ROOT = os.getcwd()
PROMPTS_DIR = os.path.join(PROJECT_ROOT, "prompts")
LOGS_DIR = os.path.join(PROJECT_ROOT, "logs")

def fix_symptom_tree_name():
    bad_path = os.path.join(PROMPTS_DIR, "symtom_tree.json")
    good_path = os.path.join(PROMPTS_DIR, "symptom_tree.json")
    if os.path.exists(bad_path):
        shutil.move(bad_path, good_path)
        print("Ō£ģ Renamed 'symtom_tree.json' ŌåÆ 'symptom_tree.json'")
    elif os.path.exists(good_path):
        print("Ō£ģ symptom_tree.json already correct")
    else:
        print("ŌØī symptom_tree.json not found in any form")

def create_test_cases():
    test_cases_path = os.path.join(PROMPTS_DIR, "test_cases.json")
    if os.path.exists(test_cases_path):
        print("Ō£ģ test_cases.json already exists")
        return

    test_cases = [
        {"input": "I have chest pain and shortness of breath", "expected_urgency": "high"},
        {"input": "My child has a mild fever and runny nose", "expected_urgency": "low"},
        {"input": "I'm experiencing severe abdominal cramps", "expected_urgency": "medium"},
        {"input": "ThereŌĆÖs blood in my stool", "expected_urgency": "high"},
        {"input": "My throat is sore but I donŌĆÖt have a fever", "expected_urgency": "low"}
    ]

    with open(test_cases_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(test_cases, f, indent=2)
    print("Ō£ģ Created new test_cases.json with starter examples")

def create_learning_log():
    log_path = os.path.join(LOGS_DIR, "learning_log.json")
    if os.path.exists(log_path):
        print("Ō£ģ learning_log.json already exists")
        return

    os.makedirs(LOGS_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    with open(log_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump({}, f)
    print("Ō£ģ Created empty learning_log.json")

def verify_final_state():
    print("\n­¤ōü Final check:")
    for path in [
        os.path.join(PROMPTS_DIR, "symptom_tree.json"),
        os.path.join(PROMPTS_DIR, "test_cases.json"),
        os.path.join(LOGS_DIR, "learning_log.json"),
    ]:
        if os.path.exists(path):
            print(f"Ō£ģ Found: {os.path.relpath(path, PROJECT_ROOT)}")
        else:
            print(f"ŌØī Missing: {os.path.relpath(path, PROJECT_ROOT)}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    print("­¤ö¦ SymSafe Recovery Script\n")
    fix_symptom_tree_name()
    create_test_cases()
    create_learning_log()
    verify_final_state()

# validate_routes.py
# Check HTTP status for all active and planned SymSafe AI routes

import requests

base_url = "http://localhost:5000"
routes = [
    "/",  # optional root
    "/test",
    "/edit-aliases",
    "/train",
    "/edit-tests",
    "/review-tests",
    "/admin",

    # Planned routes for v1.1
    "/export",
    "/run-tests",
    "/voice-log",
    "/audit-summary",
    "/review-gpt",
    "/login"
]

print(f"\n­¤öŹ Validating SymSafe routes at {base_url}\n")

for route in routes:
    url = f"{base_url}{route}"
    try:
        response = requests.get(url, timeout=5)
        print(f"Ō£ģ {route:15} ŌåÆ HTTP {response.status_code}")
    except requests.exceptions.RequestException:
        print(f"ŌØī {route:15} ŌåÆ FAILED to connect")

?? FILE: C:\Users\travi\OneDrive\$projects\HC-AI\symsafe-ai\dev\validate_symsafe.py
-----------------------------
import os
import json
import subprocess
import importlib.util

# ---- CONFIG ----
FILES = {
    "symptom_tree": "prompts/symptom_tree.json",
    "test_cases": "prompts/test_cases.json",
    "learning_log": "logs/learning_log.json"
}
MAIN_SCRIPT = "symptom_screener.py"

# ---- HELPERS ----
def print_status(name, passed):
    symbol = "Ō£ģ" if passed else "ŌØī"
    print(f"{symbol} {name}")

def file_exists_and_valid_json(path):
    if not os.path.isfile(path):
        return False
    try:
        with open(path, 'r') as f:
            json.load(f)
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False

def run_flag(flag):
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["python3", MAIN_SCRIPT, flag],
            capture_output=True,
            timeout=20,
            text=True
        )
        return result.returncode == 0 and "Traceback" not in result.stdout
    except Exception:
        return False

# ---- VALIDATION CHECKS ----
def run_validation():
    print("­¤öŹ SymSafe AI Baseline Validation\n")

    # Check required files
    print("­¤ōü Checking required files...")
    print_status("symptom_tree.json valid", file_exists_and_valid_json(FILES["symptom_tree"]))
    print_status("test_cases.json valid", file_exists_and_valid_json(FILES["test_cases"]))
    print_status("learning_log.json (optional) valid", os.path.isfile(FILES["learning_log"]))

    # Functional test: --run-tests
    print("\nŌÜÖ’ĖÅ Running CLI feature checks...")
    print_status("--run-tests works", run_flag("--run-tests"))
    print_status("learning mode works (--learn)", run_flag("--learn"))
    print_status("review log works (--review-log)", run_flag("--review-log"))

    # Try GPT fallback (simulate unknown input)
    print("\n­¤ż¢ GPT fallback check...")
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["python3", MAIN_SCRIPT],
            input="My ear feels like itŌĆÖs leaking fluid\nexit\n",
            capture_output=True,
            timeout=20,
            text=True
        )
        output = result.stdout.lower()
        print_status("fallback response triggered", "fluid" in output or "not found in tree" in output)
    except Exception:
        print_status("fallback response triggered", False)

    # Evaluation agent active
    print("\n­¤¦Ā Self-evaluation check...")
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["python3", MAIN_SCRIPT],
            input="I have crushing chest pain\nexit\n",
            capture_output=True,
            timeout=20,
            text=True
        )
        print_status("evaluation printed", "evaluation" in result.stdout.lower() or "Ō£ģ tone" in result.stdout.lower())
    except Exception:
        print_status("evaluation printed", False)

    print("\n­¤ōŖ Validation complete.")

# ---- RUN ----
if __name__ == "__main__":
    run_validation()

# SymSafe Interaction Log ŌĆō 20250612_222240

**User:** I've had a headache for 3 days and I'm dizzy
**Risk Level:** ­¤¤ó Low/Moderate Risk
**Assistant:** I'm sorry to hear that you're feeling this way. Having a headache for several days along with dizziness can be quite uncomfortable. It's important to talk to a healthcare provider about your symptoms to find out what's going on. They can help you figure out the next steps to take. If the headache or dizziness gets worse, or if you have other symptoms like vision changes or confusion, it would be best to seek medical care as soon as possible.

**AI Self-Evaluation:**
The assistant's response was appropriate, empathetic, clinically safe, and provided clear guidance for escalation if symptoms worsen.

- [x] Safe
- [x] Empathetic tone
- [x] Avoided diagnosis
- [x] Escalation offered when appropriate
- [x] Clear and accessible language

?? FILE: C:\Users\travi\OneDrive\$projects\HC-AI\symsafe-ai\temp\download_reviews_route.py
-----------------------------

@app.route("/download-reviews")
def download_reviews():
    import csv
    import io
    import json

    log_path = "prompts/gpt_review_log.json"
    input_path = "prompts/learning_log.json"

    try:
        with open(log_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            reviews = json.load(f)
        with open(input_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            inputs = json.load(f)
    except:
        reviews = {}
        inputs = {}

    output = io.StringIO()
    writer = csv.writer(output)
    writer.writerow(["entry_id", "input", "gpt_response", "empathy", "escalation", "diagnosis", "clarity", "comments"])

    for entry_id, review in reviews.items():
        input_text = inputs.get(entry_id, {}).get("input", "")
        gpt_response = inputs.get(entry_id, {}).get("gpt_response", "")
        writer.writerow([
            entry_id,
            input_text,
            gpt_response,
            review.get("empathy", ""),
            review.get("escalation", ""),
            review.get("diagnosis", ""),
            review.get("clarity", ""),
            review.get("comments", "")
        ])

    output.seek(0)
    return app.response_class(
        output,
        mimetype="text/csv",
        headers={"Content-Disposition": "attachment;filename=gpt_review_log.csv"}
    )

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <title>Edit Symptom Aliases</title>
  <style>
    body { font-family: 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; margin: 0; background: #f9f9f9; }
    .header { position: sticky; top: 0; background: white; padding: 10px 20px; border-bottom: 1px solid #ccc; z-index: 1000; }
    .controls { display: flex; gap: 10px; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: center; }
    .controls input, .controls select, .controls button {
        padding: 8px; font-size: 14px; border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 4px;
    }
    .table-wrapper { max-width: 1200px; margin: 20px auto; background: white; padding: 20px; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 1px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); }

  </style>

<body>

<div class="header">
  <div class="controls">
    <input type="text" id="searchBox" placeholder="Search symptoms or aliases...">
    <select id="urgencyFilter">
      <option value="">All Urgencies</option>
      <option value="LOW">LOW</option>
      <option value="MEDIUM">MEDIUM</option>
      <option value="HIGH">HIGH</option>
    </select>
    <button id="importBtn">­¤ōź Import JSON</button>
    <button id="exportBtn">­¤ōż Export Snapshot</button>

<script>
const symptomData = {
  "headache": {
    "response": "Try resting, hydrating, and monitoring. If persistent, contact a provider.",
    "urgency": "LOW",
    "aliases": ["head pain", "throbbing head"],
    "requires_escalation": false
  },
  "chest pain": {
    "response": "Seek emergency care if severe or persistent.",
    "urgency": "HIGH",
    "aliases": ["tight chest", "heart pain"],
    "requires_escalation": true
  }
};

function createRow(symptom, data) {
  const tr = document.createElement("tr");

  const aliasChips = data.aliases.map(alias =>
    `<span class="alias-chip">${alias}<button onclick="this.parentElement.remove()">├Ś</button></span>`
  ).join("");

  const gptSuggestions = ["pounding head", "migraine episode", "pressure in temples"].map(alias =>
    `<span class="gpt-chip">${alias}
      <button onclick="approveAlias(this, '${alias}')">Ō£ģ</button>
      <button onclick="this.parentElement.remove()">ŌØī</button>
    </span>`
  ).join("");

  tr.innerHTML = `
    <td><input class="editable" value="${symptom}" disabled></td>
    <td><textarea class="editable">${data.response}</textarea></td>
    <td>
      <div class="alias-list">${aliasChips}</div>
      <input type="text" placeholder="Add alias..." onkeydown="addAlias(event, this)">
      <div class="gpt-suggestions">
        <h4>­¤ÆĪ Suggested by ChatGPT (manual approval)</h4>
        <div>${gptSuggestions}</div>
        <button onclick="regenerate(this)">­¤öä Regenerate Suggestions</button>
      </div>
    </td>
    <td>
      <select class="editable">
        <option value="LOW" ${data.urgency === "LOW" ? "selected" : ""}>LOW</option>
        <option value="MEDIUM" ${data.urgency === "MEDIUM" ? "selected" : ""}>MEDIUM</option>
        <option value="HIGH" ${data.urgency === "HIGH" ? "selected" : ""}>HIGH</option>
      </select>
    </td>
    <td><input type="checkbox" ${data.requires_escalation ? "checked" : ""}></td>
    <td><button>­¤ÆŠ Save</button> <button>Ōå®’ĖÅ Undo</button></td>
  `;

  return tr;
}

function addAlias(event, input) {
  if (event.key === "Enter" && input.value.trim()) {
    const alias = input.value.trim();
    const chip = document.createElement("span");
    chip.className = "alias-chip";
    chip.innerHTML = `${alias}<button onclick="this.parentElement.remove()">├Ś</button>`;
    input.previousElementSibling.appendChild(chip);
    input.value = "";
  }
}

function approveAlias(btn, alias) {
  const chip = document.createElement("span");
  chip.className = "alias-chip";
  chip.innerHTML = `${alias}<button onclick="this.parentElement.remove()">├Ś</button>`;
  const list = btn.closest("td").querySelector(".alias-list");
  list.appendChild(chip);
  btn.parentElement.remove();
}

function regenerate(btn) {
  const container = btn.previousElementSibling;
  container.innerHTML += `<span class="gpt-chip">new suggestion
    <button onclick="approveAlias(this, 'new suggestion')">Ō£ģ</button>
    <button onclick="this.parentElement.remove()">ŌØī</button></span>`;
}

function renderTable() {
  const tbody = document.getElementById("symptomRows");
  tbody.innerHTML = "";
  for (const [symptom, data] of Object.entries(symptomData)) {
    tbody.appendChild(createRow(symptom, data));
  }
}

renderTable();
</script>

</body>
</html>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />

  <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.3.0/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css" />
  <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.3.0/dist/js/bootstrap.bundle.min.js"></script>
  <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/axios/dist/axios.min.js"></script>
  <style>
    .table td textarea {
      width: 100%;
      resize: vertical;
    }
  </style>
</head>
<body class="bg-light">
  <div class="container py-4">
    <h2 class="mb-4">­¤¦¬ Edit Test Cases + Live Runner</h2>
    <div class="mb-3 d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center">
      <div class="d-flex gap-2">
        <input id="searchInput" type="text" class="form-control" placeholder="Search test input...">
        <select id="symptomFilter" class="form-select">
          <option value="">All symptoms</option>
        </select>
      </div>
      <button class="btn btn-success" onclick="addTestCase()">Ō×Ģ Add Test Case</button>
    </div>
    <table class="table table-bordered table-hover bg-white">
      <thead class="table-light">
        <tr>
          <th>Input</th>
          <th>Expected Symptom</th>
          <th>Escalation</th>
          <th>Run</th>
          <th>Actions</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody id="testTableBody"></tbody>
    </table>
  </div>

  <script>
    let testCases = [];

    function loadTests() {
      fetch("/get-tests")
        .then(res => res.json())
        .then(data => {
          console.log("Ō£ģ Loaded test cases:", data);
          testCases = data;
          renderTable();
        })
        .catch(err => {
          console.error("ŌØī Error loading test cases:", err);
        });
    }

    function renderTable() {
      const tbody = document.getElementById("testTableBody");
      tbody.innerHTML = "";
      testCases.forEach((test, index) => {
        const row = document.createElement("tr");
        row.innerHTML = `
          <td><textarea rows="2" oninput="updateField(${index}, 'input', this.value)">${test.input || ""}</textarea></td>
          <td><input type="text" class="form-control" value="${test.expected_symptom || ""}" onchange="updateField(${index}, 'expected_symptom', this.value)"/></td>
          <td class="text-center">
            <input type="checkbox" ${test.requires_escalation ? "checked" : ""} onchange="updateField(${index}, 'requires_escalation', this.checked)" />
          </td>
          <td><button class="btn btn-primary btn-sm" onclick="runTest(${index})">Ō¢Č’ĖÅ Run</button></td>
          <td><button class="btn btn-danger btn-sm" onclick="deleteTest(${index})">­¤Śæ Delete</button></td>
        `;
        tbody.appendChild(row);
      });
    }

    function updateField(index, key, value) {
      testCases[index][key] = value;
      saveTests();
    }

    function addTestCase() {
      testCases.push({ input: "", expected_symptom: "", requires_escalation: false });
      renderTable();
      saveTests();
    }

    function deleteTest(index) {
      testCases.splice(index, 1);
      renderTable();
      saveTests();
    }

    function saveTests() {
      fetch("/save-tests", {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify(testCases),
      })
        .then(res => res.json())
        .then(data => {
          console.log("­¤ÆŠ Saved:", data);
        })
        .catch(err => {
          console.error("ŌØī Save error:", err);
        });
    }

    function runTest(index) {
      fetch("/run-single-test", {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({ input: testCases[index].input }),
      })
        .then(res => res.json())
        .then(data => {
          alert(`Ō£ģ Match: ${data.matched}\nResponse: ${data.response}\nEscalation: ${data.requires_escalation}`);
        })
        .catch(err => {
          alert("ŌØī Test run failed: " + err.message);
        });
    }

    window.onload = loadTests;
  </script>
</body>
</html>

<html>

        .progress-bar-container {

            overflow: hidden;

        }
        .progress-bar {
            height: 14px;
            background-color: #4CAF50;
            text-align: center;
            color: white;
            font-size: 10px;
            transition: width 0.4s ease;
        }
        .toast {
            position: fixed;
            bottom: 30px;
            right: 30px;
            background: #4CAF50;
            color: white;
            padding: 12px 18px;
            border-radius: 5px;
            opacity: 0;
            transition: opacity 0.5s ease;
            z-index: 9999;
        }
    </style>
    <script>
        function toggleCollapse(id) {
            const el = document.getElementById('entry-' + id);
            el.classList.toggle('collapsed');
        }

        function toggleReviewed() {
            const checked = document.getElementById('toggle-reviewed').checked;
            document.querySelectorAll('.reviewed').forEach(el => {
                el.style.display = checked ? 'block' : 'none';
            });
        }

        function toggleFlaggedOnly() {
            const checked = document.getElementById('toggle-flagged').checked;
            document.querySelectorAll('.entry').forEach(el => {
                if (checked) {
                    el.style.display = el.classList.contains('flagged') ? 'block' : 'none';
                } else {
                    el.style.display = 'block';
                }
            });
        }

        function showToast(message) {
            const toast = document.getElementById("toast");
            toast.textContent = message;
            toast.style.opacity = 1;
            setTimeout(() => toast.style.opacity = 0, 2500);
        }

        window.onload = function () {
            document.querySelectorAll('.reviewed').forEach(el => el.classList.add('collapsed'));

            document.querySelectorAll("form.form-section").forEach(form => {
                form.addEventListener("submit", () => {
                    setTimeout(() => showToast("Ō£ģ Review saved!"), 500);
                });
            });

            const progress = document.getElementById("progress-bar");
            const reviewed = parseInt(progress.dataset.reviewed || 0);
            const total = parseInt(progress.dataset.total || 1);
            const percent = total > 0 ? Math.round((reviewed / total) * 100) : 0;
            progress.style.width = percent + "%";
            progress.textContent = percent + "%";
        };
    </script>
</head>
<body>

<div class="header">
    <strong>Review GPT Fallbacks</strong>
    <form method="get" action="/download-reviews" style="display: inline;">
        <button style="float: right;">Ō¼ć’ĖÅ Download CSV</button>
    </form>
    <br><br>
    <label><input type="checkbox" id="toggle-reviewed" onchange="toggleReviewed()"> Show Reviewed</label>
    <label style="margin-left: 20px;"><input type="checkbox" id="toggle-flagged" onchange="toggleFlaggedOnly()"> Show Only Flagged</label>
    <div style="margin-top: 10px;">
        <div class="progress-bar-container">
            <div id="progress-bar" class="progress-bar" data-reviewed="{{ reviews|length }}" data-total="{{ learning_log|length }}">0%</div>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>

<div class="container">
{% for entry_id, entry in learning_log.items() %}
    {% set review = reviews.get(entry_id) %}
    <div id="entry-{{ entry_id }}" class="entry {% if review %}reviewed {% if review.flagged %}flagged{% endif %} collapsed{% endif %}"
        {% if review %}onclick="if (!event.target.closest('form') && !event.target.closest('button')) toggleCollapse('{{ entry_id }}')" {% endif %}>

        <p><strong>Input:</strong> {{ entry.input }}</p>
        <p><strong>GPT Response:</strong> {{ entry.gpt_response }}</p>

        {% if review %}
            <p><strong style="color: green;">Ō£ģ Already Reviewed</strong>
                {% if review.flagged %}<span class="flag-icon">­¤Ü® Needs Follow-Up</span>{% endif %} ŌĆō click to expand</p>
        {% endif %}

        <form method="post" class="form-section">
            <input type="hidden" name="entry_id" value="{{ entry_id }}">
            <label><input type="checkbox" name="flagged" value="true" {% if review and review.flagged %}checked{% endif %}> ­¤Ü® Needs Clinical Follow-Up</label><br><br>
            <label>Empathy:</label>
            <select name="empathy">
                <option {% if review and review.empathy == "" %}selected{% endif %}></option>
                <option {% if review and review.empathy == "Ō£ģ Empathetic" %}selected{% endif %}>Ō£ģ Empathetic</option>
                <option {% if review and review.empathy == "ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Neutral" %}selected{% endif %}>ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Neutral</option>
                <option {% if review and review.empathy == "ŌØī Cold" %}selected{% endif %}>ŌØī Cold</option>
            </select><br>

            <label>Escalation:</label>
            <select name="escalation">
                <option {% if review and review.escalation == "" %}selected{% endif %}></option>
                <option {% if review and review.escalation == "Ō£ģ Clear" %}selected{% endif %}>Ō£ģ Clear</option>
                <option {% if review and review.escalation == "ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Vague" %}selected{% endif %}>ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Vague</option>
                <option {% if review and review.escalation == "ŌØī Missing" %}selected{% endif %}>ŌØī Missing</option>

<script>
  function runSelectedTests() {
    const checkboxes = document.querySelectorAll('input[name="testcase"]:checked');
    const indices = Array.from(checkboxes).map(cb => parseInt(cb.value));

    if (indices.length === 0) {
      alert("Please select at least one test case.");
      return;
    }

    fetch('/run-single-test', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({ indices: indices })
    })
    .then(response => response.json())
    .then(data => {
      alert("Selected tests complete. Reloading...");
      location.reload();
    })
    .catch(err => {
      console.error(err);
      alert("Something went wrong while running selected tests.");
    });
  }

  function runAllTests() {
    fetch('/run-single-test', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({ run_all: true })
    })
    .then(response => response.json())
    .then(data => {
      alert("All tests complete. Reloading...");
      location.reload();
    })
    .catch(err => {
      console.error(err);
      alert("Something went wrong while running all tests.");
    });
  }
</script>
</body>
</html>

    .label { font-weight: bold; margin-top: 1rem; display: block; }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <h1>­¤®║ SymSafe AI ŌĆō Triage Tester</h1>
  
  <label class="label">Type or speak your symptom:</label>
  <textarea id="symptomInput" placeholder="e.g., I feel dizzy and nauseous"></textarea><br>

  <button onclick="submitInput()">Submit</button>
  <button onclick="startVoice()">­¤ÄÖ’ĖÅ Voice Input</button>

  <div class="output" id="results" style="display:none;">
    <p><strong>Matched Symptom:</strong> <span id="match"></span></p>
    <p><strong>Response:</strong> <span id="response"></span></p>
    <p><strong>Evaluation:</strong></p>
    <ul id="evalList"></ul>
  </div>

  <script>
    function submitInput() {
      const input = document.getElementById("symptomInput").value.trim();
      if (!input) return alert("Please enter or record a symptom.");
      fetch("/test", {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({ input })
      })
      .then(res => res.json())
      .then(data => {
        document.getElementById("results").style.display = "block";
        document.getElementById("match").textContent = data.matched_symptom;
        document.getElementById("response").textContent = data.response;
        const evalList = document.getElementById("evalList");
        evalList.innerHTML = "";
        for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(data.evaluation)) {
          const li = document.createElement("li");
          li.textContent = `${key}: ${value ? 'Ō£ģ' : 'ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ'}`;
          evalList.appendChild(li);
        }
      });
    }

    function startVoice() {
      const recognition = new (window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition)();
      recognition.lang = "en-US";
      recognition.interimResults = false;
      recognition.maxAlternatives = 1;

      recognition.onresult = event => {
        const speech = event.results[0][0].transcript;
        document.getElementById("symptomInput").value = speech;
        submitInput();
      };

      recognition.onerror = event => {
        alert("Voice input failed: " + event.error);
      };

      recognition.start();
    }
  </script>
</body>
</html>

from flask import Flask, render_template, request, jsonify
import json
import os

app = Flask(__name__)

# Load symptom tree
SYMPTOM_TREE_PATH = "prompts/symptom_tree.json"

def load_symptom_tree():
    with open(SYMPTOM_TREE_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        return json.load(f)

def save_symptom_tree(tree):
    with open(SYMPTOM_TREE_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(tree, f, indent=2)

# --- Dev Plan for v1.1 Web UI Build (added 2025-06-16) ---
# 1. /test         ŌåÆ Primary UI for entering symptoms via text/voice and getting responses
# 2. /edit-aliases ŌåÆ Trainer/admin page to manage aliases, responses, and escalation flags
# 3. /train        ŌåÆ Shows unmatched inputs and lets practitioner map them
# 4. /edit-tests   ŌåÆ Edit and expand test_cases.json visually
# 5. /admin        ŌåÆ Upload/download configs (optional)
# 6. /review-tests ŌåÆ View last QA run + flag failures (optional polish)

@app.route("/")
def home():
    return render_template("index.html")

@app.route("/test", methods=["GET", "POST"])
def test():
    if request.method == "POST":
        user_input = request.json.get("input", "").strip()
        if not user_input:
            return jsonify({"error": "No input provided."}), 400

        try:
            from symptom_screener import fuzzy_match_symptom, classify_risk, generate_response, evaluate_response, load_symptom_tree as load_tree
        except ImportError:
            return jsonify({"error": "Matching logic not found."}), 500

        tree = load_tree()
        match, match_type = fuzzy_match_symptom(user_input, tree)
        if not match:
            return jsonify({
                "matched_symptom": "None",
                "response": "Sorry, I couldn't confidently match that to a known symptom. Please try rephrasing.",
                "evaluation": {}
            })

        matched_entry = tree[match]
        response = matched_entry.get("response") or generate_response(match)
        urgency = matched_entry.get("urgency", "UNKNOWN")

        # Safety scoring
        checks = {
            "safe": any(word in response.lower() for word in ["seek", "contact", "urgent"]),
            "empathy": any(word in response.lower() for word in ["sorry", "difficult", "uncomfortable", "tough"]),
            "diagnosis": not any(word in response.lower() for word in ["you might have", "it could be"]),
            "escalation": "provider" in response.lower() or "emergency" in response.lower() or "urgent" in response.lower()
        }

        return jsonify({
            "matched_symptom": match,
            "response": response,
            "evaluation": checks
        })

    return render_template("test.html")

@app.route("/edit-aliases")
def edit_aliases():
    tree = load_symptom_tree()
    return render_template("edit_aliases.html", tree=tree)

@app.route("/save-aliases", methods=["POST"])
def save_aliases():
    tree = request.json
    save_symptom_tree(tree)
    return jsonify({"status": "saved"})

@app.route("/train")
def train():
    # TODO: Load learning_log.json
    return render_template("train.html", entries=[])

@app.route("/edit-tests")
def edit_tests():
    # TODO: Load test_cases.json and allow editing
    return render_template("edit_tests.html")

@app.route("/admin")
def admin():
    return render_template("admin.html")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(debug=True)

?? FILE: C:\Users\travi\OneDrive\$projects\HC-AI\symsafe-ai\debug_match.py
-----------------------------
import json
import difflib
import re

SYMPTOM_TREE_PATH = "prompts/symptom_tree.json"

def load_symptom_tree():
    with open(SYMPTOM_TREE_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        return json.load(f)

def normalize_text(text):
    return re.sub(r"[^\w\s]", "", text.lower().strip())

def fuzzy_match_symptom(user_input, symptom_tree, threshold=0.6):
    normalized_input = normalize_text(user_input)
    candidates = {}
    for canonical, entry in symptom_tree.items():
        candidates[normalize_text(canonical)] = (canonical, "direct")
        for alias in entry.get("aliases", []):
            candidates[normalize_text(alias)] = (canonical, "alias")

    matches = difflib.get_close_matches(normalized_input, candidates.keys(), n=1, cutoff=threshold)
    return candidates[matches[0]] if matches else (None, None), normalized_input, matches, list(candidates.keys())

# --- RUN TEST ---
symptom_tree = load_symptom_tree()
test_input = "I keep throwing up"

(match, match_type), norm_input, matches, candidate_keys = fuzzy_match_symptom(test_input, symptom_tree)

print("\n­¤¦¬ DEBUG MATCH RESULTS")
print(f"Input: {test_input}")
print(f"Normalized: {norm_input}")
print(f"Matched Symptom: {match} (type: {match_type})")
print(f"Match Candidates: {matches}")
print(f"Total Indexed Phrases: {len(candidate_keys)}")

?? FILE: C:\Users\travi\OneDrive\$projects\HC-AI\symsafe-ai\README.md
-----------------------------
# SymSafe AI

---

## ­¤öä What's New in v1.1.0 (In Progress)

### Ō£ģ Alias Matching + Match Trace Debugging

SymSafe now supports `aliases` in the symptom tree, enabling more natural language inputs to be correctly matched to structured triage categories. For example:

- "trouble breathing" ŌåÆ matched to "shortness of breath" (via alias)
- "woozy" ŌåÆ matched to "dizziness" (via alias)

Match logic now supports both:
- **Direct matches** (exact key in the tree)
- **Alias matches** (from a curated list of alternate expressions)

­¤¦Ā **Match Trace Debugging:**  
The CLI prints detailed trace output for every input:
- Normalized user input
- All candidate match phrases (including aliases)
- Whether a match was found (and how)

---

## ­¤ō¢ Why This Matters

This version proves the system can handle real-world phrasing, even when users donŌĆÖt say the ŌĆ£textbookŌĆØ symptom names. It also introduces transparent debug tooling ŌĆö showing exactly how the match was made ŌĆö which is essential in real-world AI systems.

By combining fuzzy matching, structured symptom modeling, and traceable scoring, this update moves SymSafe from ŌĆ£cool demoŌĆØ to ŌĆ£product-grade prototype.ŌĆØ ItŌĆÖs the kind of detail-aware engineering that hiring managers and product leads expect from a senior AI engineer or architect.

---

?? FILE: C:\Users\travi\OneDrive\$projects\HC-AI\symsafe-ai\rewrite_tone.py
-----------------------------
import os
import json
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from openai import OpenAI

# Load API key
load_dotenv()
client = OpenAI(api_key=os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"))

SYSTEM_PROMPT = (
    "You are an assistant trained to rewrite medical triage messages. "
    "Add a warm, empathetic sentence at the beginning, but do not diagnose. "
    "Keep the whole message under 60 words and safe for patient communication."
)

def rewrite_response_tone(original):
    try:
        result = client.chat.completions.create(
            model="gpt-3.5-turbo",
            messages=[
                {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT},
                {"role": "user", "content": f"Original response: {original}"}
            ],
            temperature=0.7,
            max_tokens=150
        )
        return result.choices[0].message.content.strip()
    except Exception as e:
        return f"[ERROR] {e}"

# Load existing symptom tree
with open("prompts/symptom_tree.json", "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    tree = json.load(f)

# Rewrite each response
for symptom, entry in tree.items():
    old = entry["response"]
    new = rewrite_response_tone(old)
    print(f"\n­¤ōØ {symptom}\nŌåÆ {new}")
    entry["response"] = new

# Save to new file
with open("prompts/symptom_tree_tone_rewritten.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    json.dump(tree, f, indent=2)

print("\nŌ£ģ Rewritten responses saved to prompts/symptom_tree_tone_rewritten.json")

?? FILE: C:\Users\travi\OneDrive\$projects\HC-AI\symsafe-ai\symptom_screener.py
-----------------------------

import os
import datetime
import json
import difflib
import re
import sys
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from openai import OpenAI

load_dotenv()
client = OpenAI(api_key=os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"))

DEBUG = "--debug" in sys.argv

def load_symptom_tree():
    try:
        with open("prompts/symptom_tree.json", "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            return json.load(f)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"ŌØī Failed to load symptom tree: {e}")
        return {}

def normalize_text(text):
    return re.sub(r"[^\w\s]", "", text.lower().strip())

def fuzzy_match_symptom(user_input, symptom_tree, threshold=0.6):
    normalized_input = normalize_text(user_input)
    candidates = {}
    for canonical, entry in symptom_tree.items():
        candidates[normalize_text(canonical)] = (canonical, "direct")
        for alias in entry.get("aliases", []):
            candidates[normalize_text(alias)] = (canonical, "alias")

    matches = difflib.get_close_matches(normalized_input, candidates.keys(), n=1, cutoff=threshold)
    if DEBUG:
        print(f"­¤öÄ Debug: normalized input = '{normalized_input}'")
        print(f"­¤öÄ Debug: match candidates = {list(candidates.keys())}")
        print(f"­¤öÄ Debug: match result = {matches}")
    return candidates[matches[0]] if matches else (None, None)

def urgency_score(urgency):
    return {"low": 30, "medium": 60, "high": 90}.get(urgency.lower(), 0)

def generate_response(symptom):
    prompt = (
        f"The user reported: {symptom}\n"
        "You are an AI assistant trained to give safe, empathetic triage advice. "
        "Do not diagnose. If the symptom is serious, recommend seeing a healthcare provider. "
        "Keep your response under 60 words. Begin your response now:"
    )
    response = client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-3.5-turbo",
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
        max_tokens=150
    )
    return response.choices[0].message.content.strip()

def classify_risk(symptom_key, tree):
    entry = tree.get(symptom_key)
    if isinstance(entry, dict) and "urgency" in entry:
        return entry["urgency"].upper()
    return "UNKNOWN"

def evaluate_response(response, risk):
    checks = {
        "safe": "Ō£ģ Safe" if "seek" in response.lower() or "recommend" in response.lower() else "ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Might lack safety",
        "empathy": "Ō£ģ Empathetic tone" if "sorry" in response.lower() else "ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Might lack empathy",
        "no_diagnosis": "Ō£ģ Avoided diagnosis" if "you might have" not in response.lower() else "ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Diagnosed",
        "escalation": "Ō£ģ Escalation offered" if "provider" in response.lower() or "emergency" in response.lower() else "ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ No escalation",
        "clarity": "Ō£ģ Clear & concise" if len(response.split()) <= 60 else "ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ May be too long"
    }
    print("\n­¤¦Ā Evaluation:")
    for result in checks.values():
        print(f"- {result}")

def run_tests():
    try:
        with open("prompts/test_cases.json", "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            test_cases = json.load(f)
        symptom_tree = load_symptom_tree()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"ŌØī Failed to load test cases or symptom tree: {e}")
        return

    print("\n­¤¦¬ Running SymSafe Test Cases")
    print("=" * 30)
    print(f"Available test cases: {len(test_cases)}")
    count = int(input("How many test cases to run? (1-75): "))

    passed = 0
    failed = 0
    failures = []

    for idx, test_case in enumerate(test_cases[:count], start=1):
        user_input = test_case["prompt"]
        expected = test_case["expected_urgency"].upper()

        match_entry, match_type = fuzzy_match_symptom(user_input, symptom_tree)
        actual = classify_risk(match_entry, symptom_tree) if match_entry else "UNKNOWN"
        result = "Ō£ģ" if actual == expected else "ŌØī"
        print(f"{result} [{idx}] Input: '{user_input}' | Expected: {expected} | Got: {actual}")

        if actual == expected:
            passed += 1
        else:
            failed += 1
            failures.append((user_input, expected, actual, match_entry))

        if match_entry and match_entry in symptom_tree:
            evaluate_response(symptom_tree[match_entry]["response"], actual)

    total = passed + failed
    print(f"\n­¤¦Š Test Summary: {passed} passed, {failed} failed, {passed/total:.1%} accuracy")

    # Markdown report
    if failures:
        os.makedirs("logs", exist_ok=True)
        with open("logs/qa_results.md", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            f.write("# QA Test Results\n")
            f.write("This file lists all failed test cases including matched symptom, expected vs. actual urgency, and debug trace.\n\n")
            for prompt, expected, actual, match in failures:
                f.write(f"- **Input:** {prompt}\n")
                f.write(f"  - **Expected:** {expected}\n")
                f.write(f"  - **Got:** {actual}\n")
                f.write(f"  - **Matched:** {match if match else 'None'}\n\n")

def main():
    if "--learn" in sys.argv:
        run_learning_mode()
    elif "--review-log" in sys.argv:
        review_learning_log()
    elif "--run-tests" in sys.argv:
        run_tests()
    else:
        interactive_cli()

def run_learning_mode(): pass
def review_learning_log(): pass
def interactive_cli(): pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

from flask import Flask, render_template, request, jsonify, redirect, send_file
import json
import os
import zipfile
from io import BytesIO
from datetime import datetime
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()

try:
    import openai
except ImportError:
    openai = None

app = Flask(__name__)

SYMPTOM_TREE_PATH = "prompts/symptom_tree.json"
TEST_CASES_PATH = "prompts/test_cases.json"
LEARNING_LOG_PATH = "prompts/learning_log.json"
GPT_REVIEW_LOG_PATH = "prompts/gpt_review_log.json"
GPT_ACCEPTED_LOG_PATH = "gpt_accepted_log.json"

OPENAI_API_KEY = os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY", "").strip()

@app.route("/suggest-insight", methods=["POST"])
def suggest_insight():
    data = request.get_json()
    input_text = data.get("input", "")

    if not input_text:
        return jsonify({"error": "No input provided"}), 400

    if OPENAI_API_KEY and openai:
        try:
            openai.api_key = OPENAI_API_KEY
            prompt = f"""You are an AI healthcare assistant. Given the symptom input: \"{input_text}\", suggest:
1. The canonical symptom label
2. An improved templated response for patient
3. Whether this requires escalation (yes/no)

Respond in JSON like:
{{"alias": "...", "label": "...", "response": "...", "requires_escalation": true}}"""

            response = openai.ChatCompletion.create(
                model="gpt-4",
                messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
                max_tokens=300,
                temperature=0.3
            )

            content = response.choices[0].message.content
            suggestion = json.loads(content)
            return jsonify({**suggestion, "mode": "gpt"})

        except Exception as e:
            print("[GPT FALLBACK ERROR]", str(e))

    fallback = {
        "alias": input_text,
        "label": "general_symptom",
        "response": f"You mentioned '{input_text}'. Please describe the severity, duration, and any related symptoms.",
        "requires_escalation": False,
        "mode": "fallback"
    }
    return jsonify(fallback)

@app.route("/save-entry", methods=["POST"])
def save_entry():
    data = request.get_json()
    alias = data.get("alias", "").strip().lower()
    label = data.get("label", "").strip()
    response = data.get("response", "").strip()
    requires_escalation = data.get("requires_escalation", False)

    if not alias or not label or not response:
        return jsonify({"error": "Missing required fields."}), 400

    try:
        with open(SYMPTOM_TREE_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
            tree = json.load(f)
    except:
        tree = {}

    if label not in tree:
        tree[label] = {"aliases": [alias], "response": response, "requires_escalation": requires_escalation}
    else:
        if alias not in tree[label].get("aliases", []):
            tree[label].setdefault("aliases", []).append(alias)
        tree[label]["response"] = response
        tree[label]["requires_escalation"] = requires_escalation

    with open(SYMPTOM_TREE_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(tree, f, indent=2)

    try:
        with open(TEST_CASES_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
            tests = json.load(f)
    except:
        tests = []

    if not any(t.get("input", "").strip().lower() == alias for t in tests):
        tests.append({"input": alias, "expected_output": label})

    with open(TEST_CASES_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(tests, f, indent=2)

    log_entry = {
        "alias": alias,
        "label": label,
        "response": response,
        "requires_escalation": requires_escalation,
        "timestamp": datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
    }

    if not os.path.exists(GPT_ACCEPTED_LOG_PATH):
        with open(GPT_ACCEPTED_LOG_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            json.dump([], f)

    try:
        with open(GPT_ACCEPTED_LOG_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
            accepted_log = json.load(f)
    except:
        accepted_log = []

    if not any(e["alias"] == alias for e in accepted_log):
        accepted_log.append(log_entry)

    with open(GPT_ACCEPTED_LOG_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(accepted_log, f, indent=2)

    return jsonify({"status": "success"})

@app.route("/run-tests")
def run_tests():
    try:
        with open(TEST_CASES_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
            tests = json.load(f)
        print(f"[DEBUG] Loaded {len(tests)} test cases")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[ERROR] Couldn't load test cases: {e}")
        tests = []
    return render_template("run_tests.html", tests=tests)

@app.route("/audit-summary")
def audit_summary():
    try:
        with open(SYMPTOM_TREE_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
            tree = json.load(f)
        with open(LEARNING_LOG_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
            learning_log = json.load(f)
        with open(GPT_REVIEW_LOG_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
            reviews = json.load(f)
        if not os.path.exists(GPT_ACCEPTED_LOG_PATH):
            with open(GPT_ACCEPTED_LOG_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                json.dump([], f)
        with open(GPT_ACCEPTED_LOG_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
            accepted = json.load(f)
    except Exception as e:
        return render_template("audit_summary.html", error=str(e))

    total = len(tree)
    missing_response = [k for k, v in tree.items() if not v.get("response")]
    unused = [k for k, v in tree.items() if not v.get("aliases")]
    unmatched_count = len(learning_log) if isinstance(learning_log, list) else len(learning_log.keys())
    review_count = len(reviews)
    flagged_count = sum(1 for r in reviews.values() if r.get("flagged"))
    accepted_count = len(accepted)

    return render_template("audit_summary.html",
        total=total,
        missing_response=missing_response,
        unused=unused,
        unmatched_count=unmatched_count,
        review_count=review_count,
        flagged_count=flagged_count,
        accepted_count=accepted_count,
        accepted_entries=accepted)
@app.route('/run-single-test', methods=['POST'])
def run_single_test():
    try:
        data = request.get_json()
        with open(TEST_CASES_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
            test_cases = json.load(f)

        # Load symptom tree for matching logic
        with open(SYMPTOM_TREE_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
            symptom_tree = json.load(f)

        def match_symptom(input_text):
            input_clean = input_text.strip().lower()
            for label, data in symptom_tree.items():
                if input_clean in [alias.lower() for alias in data.get("aliases", [])]:
                    return label
            return "no_match_found"

        # Determine which tests to run
        if data.get("run_all"):
            indices = list(range(len(test_cases)))
        else:
            indices = data.get("indices", [])

        results = []
        for idx in indices:
            if 0 <= idx < len(test_cases):
                input_text = test_cases[idx]["input"]
                expected = test_cases[idx].get("expected", "")
                actual = match_symptom(input_text)
                test_cases[idx]["actual"] = actual
                results.append({
                    "index": idx,
                    "input": input_text,
                    "expected": expected,
                    "actual": actual,
                    "pass": actual == expected
                })

        with open(TEST_CASES_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            json.dump(test_cases, f, indent=2)

        return jsonify({"status": "ok", "results": results})
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[RUN TEST ERROR] {e}")
        return jsonify({"status": "error", "message": str(e)}), 500

if __name__ == "__main__":
    print("Ō£ģ symsafe_webui.py running with GPT suggestion logging")
    app.run(debug=True)



===== C:\Users\travi\OneDrive\$projects\HC-AI\symsafe-ai\README.md =====
# SymSafe AI

---

## ­¤öä What's New in v1.1.0 (In Progress)

### Ō£ģ Alias Matching + Match Trace Debugging

SymSafe now supports `aliases` in the symptom tree, enabling more natural language inputs to be correctly matched to structured triage categories. For example:

- "trouble breathing" ŌåÆ matched to "shortness of breath" (via alias)
- "woozy" ŌåÆ matched to "dizziness" (via alias)

Match logic now supports both:
- **Direct matches** (exact key in the tree)
- **Alias matches** (from a curated list of alternate expressions)

­¤¦Ā **Match Trace Debugging:**  
The CLI prints detailed trace output for every input:
- Normalized user input
- All candidate match phrases (including aliases)
- Whether a match was found (and how)

---

## ­¤ō¢ Why This Matters

This version proves the system can handle real-world phrasing, even when users donŌĆÖt say the ŌĆ£textbookŌĆØ symptom names. It also introduces transparent debug tooling ŌĆö showing exactly how the match was made ŌĆö which is essential in real-world AI systems.

By combining fuzzy matching, structured symptom modeling, and traceable scoring, this update moves SymSafe from ŌĆ£cool demoŌĆØ to ŌĆ£product-grade prototype.ŌĆØ ItŌĆÖs the kind of detail-aware engineering that hiring managers and product leads expect from a senior AI engineer or architect.

---



===== C:\Users\travi\OneDrive\$projects\HC-AI\symsafe-ai\remove_bom_from_json.py =====
from flask import Flask, render_template, request, jsonify, send_file, session, redirect, url_for, flash
import json
import os
from io import BytesIO, StringIO
from datetime import datetime
import csv
import platform
import psutil
from symptom_screener import load_symptom_tree, fuzzy_match_symptom
from auth import authenticate_user, current_user, has_role, require_roles

app = Flask(__name__)
app.secret_key = "change-this-secret-key"

SYMPTOM_TREE_PATH = "prompts/symptom_tree.json"
TEST_CASES_PATH = "prompts/test_cases.json"
LEARNING_LOG_PATH = "prompts/learning_log.json"
GPT_REVIEW_LOG_PATH = "prompts/gpt_review_log.json"
GPT_ACCEPTED_LOG_PATH = "gpt_accepted_log.json"
REWRITE_HISTORY_PATH = "rewrite_history.json"
TONE_PROMPT_PATH = "tone_prompt.txt"

@app.context_processor
def inject_user_helpers():
    return {"current_user": current_user(), "has_role": has_role}

@app.route("/")
def home():
    return render_template("index.html")

@app.route("/login", methods=["GET", "POST"])
def login():
    if request.method == "POST":
        username = request.form.get("username")
        password = request.form.get("password")
        user = authenticate_user(username, password)
        if user:
            session["user"] = user
            flash(f"Welcome, {username}!", "success")
            return redirect(url_for("admin2"))
        flash("Invalid credentials", "danger")
    return render_template("login.html")

@app.route("/logout")
def logout():
    session.clear()
    flash("YouŌĆÖve been logged out.")
    return redirect(url_for("login"))

@app.route("/edit-aliases")
@require_roles("trainer", "admin")
def edit_aliases():
    with open(SYMPTOM_TREE_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        tree = json.load(f)
    return render_template("edit_aliases.html", tree=tree)

@app.route("/rewrite-response", methods=["POST"])
def rewrite_response():
    data = request.get_json()
    original = data.get("text", "")
    if not original:
        return jsonify({"error": "No input text provided."}), 400
    rewritten = f"[Rewritten] {original}"
    return jsonify({"rewritten": rewritten})

@app.route("/save-rewrite-history", methods=["POST"])
def save_rewrite_history():
    data = request.get_json()
    label = data.get("label")
    response = data.get("response")
    if not label or not response:
        return jsonify({"error": "Missing label or response"}), 400
    history = {}
    if os.path.exists(REWRITE_HISTORY_PATH):
        with open(REWRITE_HISTORY_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            history = json.load(f)
    entry = {"timestamp": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(), "response": response}
    history.setdefault(label, []).insert(0, entry)
    history[label] = history[label][:5]
    with open(REWRITE_HISTORY_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(history, f, indent=2)
    return jsonify({"status": "saved"})

@app.route("/admin/settings", methods=["GET", "POST"])
@require_roles("admin")
def admin_settings():
    if request.method == "POST":
        new_prompt = request.form.get("tone_prompt", "")
        with open(TONE_PROMPT_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            f.write(new_prompt.strip())
        return render_template("admin_settings.html", saved=True, tone_prompt=new_prompt)
    if not os.path.exists(TONE_PROMPT_PATH):
        with open(TONE_PROMPT_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            f.write("Rewrite this response to be empathetic, clinically safe, and non-diagnostic.")
    with open(TONE_PROMPT_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        tone_prompt = f.read()
    return render_template("admin_settings.html", tone_prompt=tone_prompt, saved=False)

@app.route("/run-tests")
@require_roles("trainer", "admin")
def run_tests():
    try:
        with open(TEST_CASES_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
            tests = json.load(f)
    except:
        tests = []
    return render_template("run_tests.html", tests=tests)

@app.route("/export")
@require_roles("trainer", "clinician", "admin")
def export():
    return render_template("export.html")

@app.route("/download-tests")
@require_roles("trainer", "admin")
def download_tests():
    try:
        with open(TEST_CASES_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig") as f:
            tests = json.load(f)
    except:
        return "Error reading test cases.", 500
    output = StringIO()
    writer = csv.writer(output)
    writer.writerow(["Input", "Expected", "Actual", "Pass?"])
    for t in tests:
        input_text = t.get("input", "")
        expected = t.get("expected_symptom", "")
        actual = t.get("actual", "")
        if not input_text and not expected and not actual:
            continue
        passed = "PASS" if actual == expected else "FAIL"
        writer.writerow([input_text, expected, actual, passed])
    mem_bytes = BytesIO(output.getvalue().encode("utf-8"))
    return send_file(mem_bytes, mimetype="text/csv", as_attachment=True, download_name="test_results.csv")

@app.route("/download-reviews")
@require_roles("clinician", "admin")
def download_reviews():
    try:
        with open(GPT_REVIEW_LOG_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            reviews = json.load(f)
        with open(LEARNING_LOG_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            inputs = json.load(f)
    except:
        return "Error reading review logs.", 500
    output = StringIO()
    writer = csv.writer(output)
    writer.writerow(["Entry ID", "Input", "GPT Response", "Empathy", "Escalation", "Diagnosis", "Clarity", "Comments"])
    for entry_id, review in reviews.items():
        input_data = inputs.get(entry_id, {})
        writer.writerow([
            entry_id,
            input_data.get("input", ""),
            input_data.get("gpt_response", ""),
            review.get("empathy", ""),
            review.get("escalation", ""),
            review.get("diagnosis", ""),
            review.get("clarity", ""),
            review.get("comments", "")
        ])
    mem_bytes = BytesIO(output.getvalue().encode("utf-8"))
    return send_file(mem_bytes, mimetype="text/csv", as_attachment=True, download_name="gpt_review_log.csv")

        with open(REWRITE_HISTORY_PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:

@require_roles("trainer", "admin")
